_meta.dna: google(suite) = auth(google) x drive(save) x calendar(brief) x search(trends) x export(report) x session(persist) x cross(app)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions. Questions must be EXECUTABLE — answerable by checking if something actually works, not theoretical capability.

name: Tower of Google Suite
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 31, Mumbai, Project manager at a consulting firm. Intermediate tech level. Uses Google Workspace for everything — Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Docs. Needs to connect her Google account to Solace Browser with clear OAuth scopes and the ability to see exactly what the agent can access.
questions: Can I connect my Google Workspace account through a standard Google OAuth login flow without entering my password into the Solace app directly?
Does the Google consent screen show me exactly which scopes Solace is requesting (read email, read calendar, write to Drive) before I approve?
Can I grant read-only Gmail access without granting send permission — and does the agent respect that boundary?
After authenticating, can I see my connected Google account in a Solace settings page showing the granted scopes and when the token expires?
Can I disconnect my Google account from Solace with one click and confirm that all cached tokens are deleted?
Does the authentication work with Google Workspace accounts that have admin-enforced 2FA and conditional access policies?
If my Google OAuth token expires during a recipe execution, does the system pause and ask me to re-authenticate instead of failing silently?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all Google account authentication scenarios. Aanya uses Google Workspace with enterprise policies — OAuth must be standard, scopes must be minimal, token lifecycle must be transparent. Storing passwords is a dealbreaker.

floors.persona_context: 38, Austin, Sales operations manager at a SaaS company. Intermediate tech level. Needs recipes that save reports to specific Google Drive folders, organize files by client name, and share documents with team members automatically.
questions: Can a recipe save a generated report as a Google Doc in a specific Drive folder that I designate during recipe setup?
Does the recipe create a new folder automatically if my designated folder path does not exist yet?
Can I set up a recipe that organizes downloaded files into Drive folders named by client — for example, '/Sales Reports/Acme Corp/March 2026'?
Does the system preserve the original file format when uploading (PDF stays PDF, CSV stays CSV) rather than converting everything to Google Docs format?
Can a recipe share the generated document with specific team members by email address and set their permission to 'Viewer' or 'Editor'?
Does the evidence log capture which file was saved, to which folder, and the Google Drive file ID for traceability?
Can I preview the file that the recipe is about to save to Drive before approving the write operation?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all Google Drive file operation scenarios. Ben needs automated file organization — save, folder creation, sharing, format preservation. Every file operation must be visible in the evidence trail with the Drive file ID.

floors.persona_context: 45, Montreal, Executive assistant to a CEO. Intermediate tech level. Needs a daily morning brief recipe that reads the CEO's Google Calendar, summarizes today's meetings with context about each attendee, and delivers it by 7:30am Eastern.
questions: Can a recipe read today's Google Calendar events and generate a briefing document showing meeting time, attendees, and agenda for each?
Does the briefing include context about each attendee — pulled from their LinkedIn profile or previous meeting notes in Drive?
Can I schedule the briefing recipe to run at 7:00 AM Eastern and deliver the summary to the CEO's Gmail inbox by 7:30 AM?
Does the briefing highlight schedule conflicts — for example, two meetings overlapping from 2-3 PM — with a suggested resolution?
Can the recipe pull preparation materials linked in calendar event descriptions and include a summary of each document?
Does the briefing show travel time between meetings if they are at different physical locations?
Can I customize the briefing format — for example, bullet points instead of paragraphs, with the most important meeting at the top?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all calendar briefing scenarios. Christine serves a CEO — the briefing must be accurate, contextual, and on time. A wrong meeting time or missing attendee is a professional embarrassment. Timezone handling across international meetings is critical.

floors.persona_context: 28, Portland, SEO specialist at a digital marketing agency. Expert tech level. Needs a recipe that uses Google Search to monitor trending topics, competitor rankings, and SERP changes for client keywords — then exports structured data for analysis.
questions: Can a recipe perform Google searches for a list of 20 client keywords and capture the top 10 results for each including title, URL, and snippet?
Does the recipe capture Google's 'People Also Ask' section for each keyword so I can identify content gap opportunities?
Can I schedule a weekly recipe that compares this week's search results to last week's and highlights ranking changes?
Does the recipe output structured JSON or CSV data that I can import directly into my SEO analysis tools?
Can the recipe detect if a competitor's page has entered or exited the top 10 for a tracked keyword and flag it?
Does the system handle Google's rate limiting gracefully — spacing out searches to avoid being blocked?
Can I save the raw SERP screenshots alongside the structured data as evidence of what Google showed on that date?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all Google Search trend extraction scenarios. Ryan needs structured, repeatable SERP data — not one-off searches. Rate limiting, historical comparison, and evidence capture are essential for professional SEO work.

floors.persona_context: 40, Miami, Account manager at a PR agency. Intermediate tech level. Needs a recipe that pulls data from Gmail (client emails), Drive (media lists), and Calendar (meeting notes) to generate a weekly client activity report as a formatted Google Doc.
questions: Can a recipe pull emails from a specific Gmail label, meeting notes from Calendar events, and documents from a Drive folder to compile a single report?
Does the generated report use a Google Docs template I provide — with my agency's logo, fonts, and section headings?
Can the recipe count and categorize client interactions by type (email, meeting, phone call) for the weekly summary?
Does the report include a timeline view showing client touchpoints chronologically throughout the week?
Can I review and edit the generated report in the approval dialog before it is saved to Drive and shared with the client?
Does the evidence trail capture which emails, events, and files were used to generate the report — so I can trace any data point back to its source?
Can the recipe generate reports for all 8 of my clients in a batch run, each customized with their specific data?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all multi-source report generation scenarios. Elena needs cross-app data aggregation — Gmail + Drive + Calendar into one document. The report must be client-presentable (formatted, branded) not a raw data dump.

floors.persona_context: 50, Osaka, IT administrator at a manufacturing company. Expert tech level. Manages 30 employees who use Solace Browser with their Google accounts. Needs to monitor active sessions, troubleshoot authentication issues, and ensure no stale sessions persist.
questions: Can I see a dashboard showing all 30 employees' active Google sessions with their connection status, token expiry, and last activity time?
Does the session manager detect stale sessions — accounts where the Google token has expired but the session was not properly closed?
Can I force-disconnect a specific employee's Google session from the admin panel if they forgot to log out?
Does the system handle Google Workspace session policy changes (like reduced session length) without requiring employees to manually reconfigure?
Can I see error logs for failed Google authentication attempts to troubleshoot why a specific employee cannot connect?
Does the session manager track how many Google API calls each employee's recipes are making to identify potential quota issues?
If Google revokes our organization's API access, does the system notify all affected employees with clear instructions on how to re-authorize?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all Google session management scenarios. Tanaka manages 30 users — he needs centralized visibility, stale session detection, force-disconnect, and quota monitoring. Silent session failures mean recipes run against stale data.

floors.persona_context: 36, Edinburgh, Business analyst at a management consulting firm. Expert tech level. Needs recipes that chain Google apps together — search findings go into a Doc, Doc link goes into a Calendar invite, Calendar attendees get a Drive folder shared.
questions: Can a recipe chain actions across Google apps — for example, search results saved to a Doc, then the Doc link inserted into a Calendar event?
Does data flow between recipe steps preserve formatting — for example, a table copied from Search results appears as a table in the Google Doc, not plain text?
Can a recipe read attendees from a Calendar event and automatically share a specific Drive folder with all of them?
Does the cross-app recipe handle permission errors gracefully — for example, if one attendee's email address is not a valid Google account?
Can I see a data flow diagram in the recipe editor showing how data moves between Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Docs in my recipe?
Does the evidence trail capture each cross-app handoff — showing the data that left one app and arrived at the next?
Can I test the cross-app flow with a dry run that shows me what would happen at each step without actually modifying any Google data?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to cover all cross-app data flow scenarios. Sarah needs seamless Google-to-Google automation — the value is in chaining apps together, not using them in isolation. Permission handling across apps and data format preservation are the hardest problems.
